chunks:
# ==============================================================================
# IVD Recipe: Agent Rules — IVD Enforcement in AI Agent Instructions
# ==============================================================================
# Pattern for embedding IVD verification into the AI agent's instruction file
# (.cursorrules, .clinerules, Copilot instructions, system prompts, etc.).
#
# The agent instruction file is the RUNTIME enforcement point for IVD.
# Without these rules, the agent may write intent, implement against it,
# but skip the verification step — silently breaking the IVD loop.
#
# Status: Canonical
# Version: 1.2
# Created: 2026-02-09
# ==============================================================================

recipe:
  name: "Agent Rules for IVD Enforcement"
  version: "1.2"
  category: "agent_patterns"
  status: "canonical"
  created: "2026-02-09"

  description: |
    AI agents (Cursor, Copilot, Cline, Aider, Claude, GPT) follow instruction
    files that define how they behave. IVD's Principle 6 workflow requires the
    agent to verify its implementation against the intent artifact's constraints.
    But without explicit rules in the agent's instruction file, nothing ENFORCES
    that verification step. The agent implements and moves on.

    This recipe provides the IVD rules block to embed in any AI agent's
    instruction file. It makes the post-implementation verification protocol
    mandatory — the agent must re-read the intent, diff each change against
    constraints, check every test path, and report results before declaring
    the implementation complete.

  problem_solved: |
    AI agent implements from an intent artifact but skips verification.
    Constraints exist with test paths, but the agent never goes back to check
    them. Result: implementation drifts from intent silently — the exact
    problem IVD was designed to prevent.

    Root cause: the verification step in IVD's workflow is aspirational
    ("AI verifies") but not enforced. The agent's instruction file is the
    only place where enforcement can happen at runtime.

  applies_to:
    - "Any project using IVD with an AI coding agent"
    - "Cursor IDE (.cursorrules)"
    - "Cline (.clinerules)"
    - "GitHub Copilot (system instructions)"
    - "Aider (.aider.conf.yml)"
    - "Any LLM-based agent with configurable instructions"

  when_to_use:
    - "Installing IVD in a project (add during ivd_init)"
    - "Agent implements from intent artifacts but doesn't verify"
    - "Post-implementation bugs trace back to unverified constraints"
    - "Team wants to enforce IVD discipline at the agent level"

  when_NOT_to_use:
    - "No AI agent is used (pure human development)"
    - "Project doesn't use IVD intent artifacts"

# ==============================================================================
# THE POST-IMPLEMENTATION VERIFICATION PROTOCOL
# ==============================================================================

verification_protocol:
  name: "Post-Implementation Verification Protocol"
  trigger: "After ANY implementation derived from an intent artifact"

  description: |
    After implementing code, documentation, or any artifac

erified constraints"
    - "Team wants to enforce IVD discipline at the agent level"

  when_NOT_to_use:
    - "No AI agent is used (pure human development)"
    - "Project doesn't use IVD intent artifacts"

# ==============================================================================
# THE POST-IMPLEMENTATION VERIFICATION PROTOCOL
# ==============================================================================

verification_protocol:
  name: "Post-Implementation Verification Protocol"
  trigger: "After ANY implementation derived from an intent artifact"

  description: |
    After implementing code, documentation, or any artifact from an IVD intent,
    the AI agent MUST execute this 4-step audit before declaring the work done.
    This is Principle 4 (Continuous Verification) made concrete.

  steps:
    - step: 1
      name: "Re-read the literal intent"
      action: |
        Open and re-read the intent artifact file from disk. Do NOT rely on
        memory or context from earlier in the conversation. The intent may have
        been updated, or the agent's memory may have drifted.
      why: |
        This is cognitively necessary, not just a best practice. The lost-in-the-middle
        effect (2024 research, GPT-4/Claude 3): LLMs degrade in accuracy for information
        positioned in the middle of long contexts — effective context window can be 99%
        smaller than advertised. Information discussed early in a session has degraded
        attention weight by implementation time. Re-reading repositions the intent at the
        top of the context stack where the attention mechanism weights it highest. The file
        is the source of truth; the agent's context memory is not.

    - step: 2
      name: "Diff each change against constraints"
      action: |
        For every change made during implementation, check: does this change
        satisfy, violate, or not affect each constraint in the intent artifact?
        List each constraint and its status.
      why: "Changes may satisfy some constraints while silently violating others."

    - step: 3
      name: "Check verification criteria"
      action: |
        For each constraint that has a 'test' field, verify:
        - Does the test path exist?
        - Can the test be run?
        - Does the implementation pass the test?
        If the test can't be run (e.g., manual review), note it and describe
        what a reviewer should check.
      why: "Test paths are the executable understanding. Unchecked tests are unverified claims."

    - step: 4
      name: "Report results"
      action: |
        Produce a structured report:
        - For each constraint: PASS / FAIL / NEEDS_REVIEW
        - For each FAIL: what specifically doesn't match and what to fix
        - For each NEEDS_REVIEW: what a human should verify
        Do not declare implementation complete until all constraints are PASS
        or explicitly acknowledged as NEEDS_REVIEW.
      why: "Structured reporting makes verification visible and auditable."

  failure_mode: |
    If the agent skips this protocol, the IVD loop is broken:
    Intent exists → Implementation exists → But no verification connects the

: 4
      name: "Report results"
      action: |
        Produce a structured report:
        - For each constraint: PASS / FAIL / NEEDS_REVIEW
        - For each FAIL: what specifically doesn't match and what to fix
        - For each NEEDS_REVIEW: what a human should verify
        Do not declare implementation complete until all constraints are PASS
        or explicitly acknowledged as NEEDS_REVIEW.
      why: "Structured reporting makes verification visible and auditable."

  failure_mode: |
    If the agent skips this protocol, the IVD loop is broken:
    Intent exists → Implementation exists → But no verification connects them.
    The intent becomes decoration, not a contract. This is the exact failure
    mode IVD was designed to prevent.

# ==============================================================================
# AGENT RULES BLOCK — COPY INTO YOUR AGENT INSTRUCTION FILE
# ==============================================================================

agent_rules_block:
  description: |
    Copy this block into your agent's instruction file (.cursorrules,
    .clinerules, Copilot instructions, etc.). Adapt the format to your agent
    but keep the rules intact.

  cursorrules_format: |
    # ==============================================================================
    # IVD: Intent-Verified Development Rules
    # ==============================================================================
    # These rules enforce the IVD verification loop. Do not remove them.
    # Reference: ivd_system_intent.yaml, framework.md (Principle 4, Principle 6)

    ## IVD Rule 1: Intent Before Implementation

    Before implementing any feature, fix, or change that has an associated
    intent artifact (*_intent.yaml):
    1. READ the intent artifact first
    2. UNDERSTAND the constraints and their test paths
    3. IMPLEMENT against the constraints
    4. VERIFY using the Post-Implementation Verification Protocol (IVD Rule 2)

    If no intent artifact exists for the change, note it. For significant changes,
    create one using ivd_scaffold before implementing.

    ## IVD Rule 2: Post-Implementation Verification Protocol

    After ANY implementation derived from an intent artifact, execute this
    4-step audit BEFORE declaring the work done:

    1. RE-READ the literal intent artifact from disk (not from memory)
    2. DIFF each change against each constraint — does it satisfy, violate, or
       not affect the constraint?
    3. CHECK every constraint that has a 'test' field — does the implementation
       pass? If the test can't be run, note what a reviewer should verify.
    4. REPORT structured results: for each constraint, PASS / FAIL / NEEDS_REVIEW

    Do not declare implementation complete until all constraints are PASS or
    explicitly acknowledged as NEEDS_REVIEW with justification.

    ## IVD Rule 3: Constraint Tests Are Mandatory

    Every constraint in an intent artifact MUST have a 'test' field. If you
    encounter a constraint without a test, add one before implementing.
    A constraint without a test is an unverifiable claim — it violates
    Principle 2 (Understanding Must Be Executable).

    ## IVD Rule 4: St

pass? If the test can't be run, note what a reviewer should verify.
    4. REPORT structured results: for each constraint, PASS / FAIL / NEEDS_REVIEW

    Do not declare implementation complete until all constraints are PASS or
    explicitly acknowledged as NEEDS_REVIEW with justification.

    ## IVD Rule 3: Constraint Tests Are Mandatory

    Every constraint in an intent artifact MUST have a 'test' field. If you
    encounter a constraint without a test, add one before implementing.
    A constraint without a test is an unverifiable claim — it violates
    Principle 2 (Understanding Must Be Executable).

    ## IVD Rule 4: Stress-Test Intent Before Implementing

    After the human reviews and approves the intent, and BEFORE you start
    implementing, adversarially probe the intent for completeness:

    1. CONSTRAINT COMPLETENESS: What input breaks this? What edge case isn't
       covered by any constraint? Are there boundary conditions missing?
    2. IMPLEMENTATION ANTICIPATION: What decisions will the code force that
       this intent doesn't address? Integration points? Data shapes? Error
       paths? Ordering dependencies?
    3. ASSUMPTION CHALLENGE: What am I assuming about the environment, data,
       dependencies, or user behavior that isn't documented in the intent?

    If gaps are found, update the intent (add constraints, test paths, or
    clarifying notes) BEFORE implementing. For significant changes, flag
    them for the human to re-review.

    Skip this step only for trivial changes where the intent is a single
    constraint with an obvious implementation.

    ## IVD Rule 5: Empirical Refinement During Implementation

    When implementation reveals that the intent's assumptions are wrong (API
    returns a different format, model accuracy differs from benchmarks, latency
    exceeds documented limits, edge cases the research didn't cover):

    1. STOP — do not continue implementing against a stale assumption. The intent
       is the contextual knowledge spine; if it's wrong, everything built on it
       will be wrong.
    2. RECORD — document what the intent assumed vs. what you observed. This is
       new contextual knowledge.
    3. UPDATE — revise the intent artifact on disk: fix the constraint, adjust the
       threshold, add a new constraint for the discovered edge case, update the
       rationale with empirical evidence.
    4. ENRICH — if the discovery reveals a knowledge gap, pull more contextual
       knowledge into the session (API documentation, model cards, actual error
       responses, performance benchmarks).
    5. CONTINUE — re-read the updated intent (Rule 2, Step 1) and continue
       implementing from the corrected version.

    If the discovery changes scope significantly (what was a simple API call now
    requires retry logic, circuit breakers, and fallback handling), flag for human
    review before continuing.

    The stress test (Rule 4) anticipates. Empirical refinement discovers. Together
    they close the loop.

  clinerules_format: |
    # IVD Verification Rules
    # Before implementing from an intent artifact:
    # - Stress-test: what edge cases, gaps, assumptions a

al error
       responses, performance benchmarks).
    5. CONTINUE — re-read the updated intent (Rule 2, Step 1) and continue
       implementing from the corrected version.

    If the discovery changes scope significantly (what was a simple API call now
    requires retry logic, circuit breakers, and fallback handling), flag for human
    review before continuing.

    The stress test (Rule 4) anticipates. Empirical refinement discovers. Together
    they close the loop.

  clinerules_format: |
    # IVD Verification Rules
    # Before implementing from an intent artifact:
    # - Stress-test: what edge cases, gaps, assumptions are missing?
    # - Update intent with found gaps before implementing
    # During implementation:
    # - If reality contradicts the intent (API format, accuracy, latency differs):
    #   STOP → RECORD finding → UPDATE intent on disk → ENRICH context → CONTINUE
    # After implementing from an intent artifact:
    # 1. Re-read the intent file (not from memory)
    # 2. Check each change against each constraint
    # 3. Verify every constraint.test path
    # 4. Report: PASS / FAIL / NEEDS_REVIEW per constraint
    # Do not mark task complete until all constraints verified.

  copilot_format: |
    When implementing from IVD intent artifacts (*_intent.yaml):
    - Read the intent artifact before starting
    - Before implementing, stress-test the intent: what edge cases, gaps, or
      assumptions are missing? Update intent with found gaps first.
    - During implementation, if reality contradicts the intent (API format,
      accuracy, latency differs from what the intent says): STOP, update the
      intent on disk with the empirical finding, then continue from the updated
      version
    - After implementation, re-read the intent from disk
    - Check each constraint: does implementation satisfy it?
    - For constraints with test fields, verify the test passes
    - Report pass/fail per constraint before declaring done

# ==============================================================================
# EXAMPLE: VERIFICATION PROTOCOL IN ACTION
# ==============================================================================

example:
  scenario: "Agent implements a scoring function from an intent artifact"

  intent_constraints:
    - name: "score_threshold"
      requirement: "score >= 0.70"
      test: "tests/test_scoring.py::test_threshold"
    - name: "precision_minimum"
      requirement: "precision >= 0.80"
      test: "tests/test_scoring.py::test_precision"
    - name: "admin_only"
      requirement: "Only admin users can access scoring API"
      test: "tests/test_auth.py::test_admin_required"

  agent_implements: "Scoring function with threshold 0.70 and API endpoint"

  verification_report: |
    ## Post-Implementation Verification Report
    Intent: agent/lead_scorer/lead_scorer_intent.yaml v3

    | Constraint        | Status       | Evidence                                    |
    |-------------------|--------------|---------------------------------------------|
    | score_threshold   | PASS         | threshold = 0.70 in scoring.py L15          |
    | precision_minimum | NEEDS_REVIEW | Requires

only"
      requirement: "Only admin users can access scoring API"
      test: "tests/test_auth.py::test_admin_required"

  agent_implements: "Scoring function with threshold 0.70 and API endpoint"

  verification_report: |
    ## Post-Implementation Verification Report
    Intent: agent/lead_scorer/lead_scorer_intent.yaml v3

    | Constraint        | Status       | Evidence                                    |
    |-------------------|--------------|---------------------------------------------|
    | score_threshold   | PASS         | threshold = 0.70 in scoring.py L15          |
    | precision_minimum | NEEDS_REVIEW | Requires running test suite with test data   |
    | admin_only        | FAIL         | No auth check on /api/score endpoint         |

    Action needed:
    - admin_only: Add auth middleware to /api/score endpoint
    - precision_minimum: Run tests/test_scoring.py::test_precision after deploying

  outcome: |
    Without the protocol: agent ships the endpoint without auth check.
    With the protocol: agent catches the missing auth check before declaring done.
    The constraint existed in the intent — the protocol ensures the agent reads it.

# ==============================================================================
# INTEGRATION WITH IVD TOOLING
# ==============================================================================

integration:
  ivd_init:
    description: "When ivd_init scans a project and finds .cursorrules or equivalent, suggest adding the IVD rules block"
    trigger: "ivd_init detects agent instruction file"
    action: "Append IVD rules block to scan summary and next_steps"

  ivd_validate:
    description: "ivd_validate already checks for constraints without test fields. The verification protocol depends on test fields existing."
    relationship: "Validation ensures the intent is verifiable; the protocol ensures the agent actually verifies."

  ivd_scaffold:
    description: "Scaffolded intents include constraints with test placeholders. The protocol ensures the agent fills them in and checks them."
    relationship: "Scaffold provides structure; the protocol provides enforcement."

# ==============================================================================
# COMMON FAILURES
# ==============================================================================

common_failures:
  - name: "Agent Skips Re-Read"
    description: "Agent implements from memory of the intent, not from the actual file"
    symptom: "Implementation matches what agent remembers, not what intent says (intent may have been updated)"
    root_cause: "Verification Protocol Step 1 skipped — agent didn't re-read from disk"
    solution: "Verification Protocol Step 1: always re-read from disk, not from context/memory"

  - name: "Partial Constraint Check"
    description: "Agent checks some constraints but misses others"
    symptom: "Some constraints satisfied, others silently violated"
    root_cause: "Verification Protocol Step 2 incomplete — agent didn't enumerate all constraints"
    solution: "Verification Protocol Step 2: list EVERY constraint with status, not just the ones the agent remembers"

  - name: "Aspirational Te

says (intent may have been updated)"
    root_cause: "Verification Protocol Step 1 skipped — agent didn't re-read from disk"
    solution: "Verification Protocol Step 1: always re-read from disk, not from context/memory"

  - name: "Partial Constraint Check"
    description: "Agent checks some constraints but misses others"
    symptom: "Some constraints satisfied, others silently violated"
    root_cause: "Verification Protocol Step 2 incomplete — agent didn't enumerate all constraints"
    solution: "Verification Protocol Step 2: list EVERY constraint with status, not just the ones the agent remembers"

  - name: "Aspirational Test Paths"
    description: "Constraint has a test field but the test doesn't exist or can't run"
    symptom: "Agent reports PASS but test was never actually run"
    root_cause: "Verification Protocol Step 3 skipped — agent assumed test exists without checking"
    solution: "Verification Protocol Step 3: verify test path exists and can run before reporting PASS"

  - name: "No Report, Just Ship"
    description: "Agent implements and declares done without structured verification"
    symptom: "No audit trail; failures discovered later by humans or in production"
    root_cause: "Verification Protocol Step 4 skipped — no structured report produced"
    solution: "Verification Protocol Step 4: require structured report before declaring complete"

  - name: "Implementing Against Stale Assumptions"
    description: "Agent discovers that reality contradicts the intent (API format differs, accuracy is lower) but continues implementing against the original intent"
    symptom: "Implementation has workarounds, hardcoded fixes, or silent data transformations that the intent doesn't capture; future agents re-read the intent and build wrong"
    root_cause: "Empirical Refinement (Rule 5) skipped — agent treated the discovery as a code problem instead of an intent problem"
    solution: "Rule 5: STOP, RECORD finding, UPDATE intent on disk, ENRICH context, CONTINUE from corrected intent"

# ==============================================================================
# RELATIONSHIP TO OTHER PATTERNS
# ==============================================================================

relationships:
  - pattern: "Principle 4 (Continuous Verification)"
    relationship: "This recipe IS Principle 4 made concrete for AI agents. The principle says 'verify continuously'; this recipe defines what that means at the implementation level."

  - pattern: "Principle 6 (AI as Understanding Partner)"
    relationship: "The Principle 6 workflow ends with 'AI verifies.' This recipe defines what that step concretely requires."

  - pattern: "constraints[].test field"
    relationship: "The test field in constraints is what the verification protocol checks. Without test fields, the protocol can't verify. Without the protocol, test fields are aspirational."

  - pattern: "Principle 3 (Bidirectional Synchronization)"
    relationship: "Rule 5 (Empirical Refinement) is Principle 3 extended to a new sync direction: reality → intent. When implementation reveals that the intent's assumptions are wrong, the agent syncs empirical evidence back into the i

ip: "The Principle 6 workflow ends with 'AI verifies.' This recipe defines what that step concretely requires."

  - pattern: "constraints[].test field"
    relationship: "The test field in constraints is what the verification protocol checks. Without test fields, the protocol can't verify. Without the protocol, test fields are aspirational."

  - pattern: "Principle 3 (Bidirectional Synchronization)"
    relationship: "Rule 5 (Empirical Refinement) is Principle 3 extended to a new sync direction: reality → intent. When implementation reveals that the intent's assumptions are wrong, the agent syncs empirical evidence back into the intent before continuing."

  - pattern: "project_context.code_rules"
    relationship: "The agent instruction file (.cursorrules) is listed in project_context.code_rules. This recipe adds IVD-specific content to that file."

# ==============================================================================
# CHANGELOG
# ==============================================================================

changelog:
  - version: "1.2"
    date: "2026-02-09"
    change: "Added IVD Rule 5: empirical refinement during implementation. Updated clinerules and Copilot formats."
    reason: |
      Gap identified: when implementation reveals that the intent's assumptions don't match
      reality (API returns different format, model accuracy differs, latency exceeds limits),
      no agent rule existed to stop, update the intent, and continue from the corrected
      version. Rule 4 (stress test) anticipates gaps before implementation; Rule 5 catches
      gaps that only running the code reveals. Together they close the loop.

  - version: "1.1"
    date: "2026-02-09"
    change: "Added IVD Rule 4: stress-test intent before implementing. Updated clinerules and Copilot formats."
    reason: |
      Gap identified: implementation reveals gaps not covered by intent, and adversarial
      stress-testing of intent surfaces flaws cheaply. The P6 workflow now includes a
      stress-test step between human review and implementation. Rule 4 enforces this
      at the agent level.

  - version: "1.0"
    date: "2026-02-09"
    change: "Initial recipe for IVD agent rules and post-implementation verification protocol"
    reason: |
      Gap identified by developer: AI agent implemented from intent artifact but skipped
      verification. Constraints existed with test paths but agent never checked them.
      Root cause: IVD Principle 4 says "continuous verification" but doesn't prescribe
      a concrete mechanism. The agent instruction file is the enforcement point, but
      IVD didn't provide rules for it. This recipe fills both gaps.

. The agent instruction file is the enforcement point, but
      IVD didn't provide rules for it. This recipe fills both gaps.
